ab: mau na pararawa i dekalr ni malri.
ch: 我的爸爸是部落的獵人。
en: My dad is a hunter of the tribe.

ab: makiteng ku diya mu, i malri di i ama na maralrak kani malri, nu ’aremeng mukakuwa i calun, miinada da tadaw di kuwang.
ch: 我小的時候，爸爸和叔叔會在晚上去野外，帶著獵刀和獵槍。
en: When I was little, my dad and uncle would go out at night with hunting knives and guns.

ab: nu ’uninan lra mu, tu adayaw nantu rawa puruma’.
ch: 天亮時，他們就會帶著獵到的獵物回來。
en: At daybreak, they returned with their prey.

ab: nu eman mau na vavuy, nu eman mau na rava, nu eman mirawa da tuu’.
ch: 有時候是山豬、有時候是飛鼠，偶爾也會獵到雉雞。
en: Sometimes it was a boar, sometimes a flying squirrel, and occasionally a pheasant.

ab: tu karelrawalrawa’ay i salikid kana dekalr, pu’acar kana dinkalranan.
ch: 他們會在村口將獵物分好，分享給族人。
en: They would divide the prey at the entrance of the village and share it with the tribe.

ab: marayas ku makiwalray kani malri, maranger ku kurelrang mukuwa pa’alrupa.
ch: 我每次都會吵著爸爸，要跟著爸爸去打獵。
en: Every time I quarreled with my father, I wanted to go hunting with my father.

ab: nu ma’idang ku lra tuy pilralrangaw ku lra mukuwa kema i malri.
ch: 爸爸說我長大了他會帶我一起去。
en: Dad said that he would take me with me when I grew up.

ab: na ma’inayan i dekalr sayhu penecir da rawa, sayhu demaway da putiru’an di cine’ece’an da varasa’ na pecir.
ch: 部落的男人也會用陷阱抓獵物，他們會製作套索陷阱和石壓式陷阱。
en: The men of the tribe also trap their prey, making lasso traps and rock traps.

ab: sayhu demaway i malri kana putiru’an na pecir, marengay i malri mu, ini mau na ’a’evangen kana tuu’, ta tarena’uway tu dinapalran na rawa, na pecir mu ta ca’anay kanantu kakalrangan na dalan kema.
ch: 我的爸爸很會製作套索陷阱，爸爸說，這是用來捕抓雉雞的，我們要仔細觀察獵物的腳印，陷阱要放在獵物走的路上。
en: My dad is very good at making lasso traps. Dad said that this is used to catch pheasants. We have to carefully observe the footprints of the prey, and the traps should be placed on the path of the prey.

ab: kana ku marangeran pakunamun kurelrang kana ma’idangan mukuwa pa’alrupa, kana wariwari diya menadanadam ku demaway kana pecir. adi sawariyan nangku pecir tatena’ pateka lra rawa.
ch: 為了想要趕快跟長輩一起去打獵，我每天練習製作陷阱，很快地我的陷阱就真的抓到獵物了。
en: In order to go hunting with adults as soon as possible, I practiced making traps every day, and soon my traps can really catch prey.

ab: mena’u lra i malri mu, marengay kaningku, nu kadaidan lra kamau u na tatena’ na pararawa kema.
ch: 爸爸看到後對我說，你將來一定會是一個真正的獵人。
en: Dad saw it and told me that I will be a very good hunter in the future.

ab: ma’idang ku lra harem, ku padekaw nangku kadalis di valru’, marelrang ku kani malri mukuwa pa’alrupa.
ch: 現在我長大了，我背起我的弓和箭矢，跟著爸爸一起去打獵。
en: Now that I have grown up, I carry my bow and arrows and go hunting with my father.

ab: muruma’ mi i dekalr mu, naniyam rawa niyam pu’acaranay kana dinkalranan.
ch: 回到部落時將獵到的獵物和族人們分享。
en: When returning to the village, share the hunted prey with the tribe.

ab: marengay i malri, sasungar ta kanantu ipuvati da temuwamuwan, tu iveray kanta da rawa karuwa ta diya lremawa’.
ch: 爸爸說，我們要感謝祖靈賜福，給我們的獵物還能夠分享 。
en: Dad said, we want to thank the ancestors for their blessings, so that we have a lot of prey to harvest.
